title: Deaths from road injuries
subtitle: Annual number of deaths from road injuries. Road injuries include deaths of drivers, passengers, pedestrians and cyclists.
caption:
Deaths from road injuries, 2023

Annual number of deaths from road injuries. Road injuries include deaths of drivers, passengers, pedestrians and cyclists.

The choropleth map displays the annual number of deaths from road injuries across countries in 2023, using a color scale that ranges from light beige for zero deaths to deep maroon for 100,000 or more deaths. The visualization shows a wide variation in mortality counts, with the highest values concentrated in a few populous nations. China and India appear in the darkest maroon band, indicating each recorded 100,000 or more road injury deaths. The United States, Brazil, and Russia fall into the next tier, shaded in a medium-dark red that corresponds to the 30,000 to 100,000 range. Mexico, Indonesia, and several countries in sub-Saharan Africa — including Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Ethiopia — are colored in a lighter orange-red, placing them in the 10,000 to 30,000 band. Many countries in Western Europe, such as France, Germany, and the United Kingdom, along with Canada and Australia, are shaded in pale orange, signaling death counts between 1,000 and 3,000. The lightest beige tones, representing fewer than 1,000 deaths, cover most of Scandinavia, New Zealand, and numerous small island nations. Greenland and a handful of other territories are marked with diagonal hatching to denote no available data. The geographic pattern shows that the largest absolute numbers of road injury deaths occur in Asia and the Americas, while lower counts dominate Oceania and northern Europe. The distribution of colors reveals that road injury mortality is not evenly spread but clusters in specific regions with large populations and varying infrastructure conditions.
